properties.notes: Union of the Estonia governorate uyezds and the northern Livonian uyezds; the Valga line remains a documented approximation.
Union of southern Livonian, Kurland, and Latvian Latgale uyezds; the Valga and eastern treaty lines remain approximate at uyezd resolution.
Kovno uyezd union used for the de facto Lithuanian state; the contested Vilna region is intentionally excluded and the frontier is not represented below uyezd resolution.
The 1897 St. Petersburg governorate geometry is retained as the real vector basis and renamed; later municipal changes are not separately digitized.
Retained IISH province geometry.
Remaining Novgorod uyezds after the Cherepovets transfer.
Union of the five transferred Novgorod uyezds.
Whole-uyezd union from Arkhangelsk and Olonets provides a real vector basis; several commune boundary adjustments cut below uyezd level and remain approximate.
Olonets remainder after the Karelian Labour Commune transfer, represented by whole uyezds.
Kola uyezd is the source-feature basis for the Murmansk unit; coastal administrative refinements are not digitized.
Arkhangelsk remainder after Kola, Kem, and the Pechora partition used for the later Komi-Zyryan transition.
North Dvina remainder after separating the Yarensk and Ust-Sysolsk geographic partition used for the August Komi-Zyryan transition.

administrativePeriods.administrativeType: district territory across Arkhangelsk and North Dvina governorates
administrativePeriods.displayName: Komi districts of Arkhangelsk and North Dvina

properties.notes: Stable geographic partition from Pechora, Yarensk, and Ust-Sysolsk source features. Before August it represents districts administered through Arkhangelsk and North Dvina; from August it represents the Komi-Zyryan Autonomous Oblast.
Vologda remainder after the North Dvina transfer.
Retained IISH province geometry.
Retained IISH province geometry.
Union of the five transferred Yaroslavl uyezds.
Yaroslavl remainder after the Rybinsk split.
Core province union from Kineshma, Yuryevets, and Shuya uyezds; sub-uyezd transfers remain approximate.
Vladimir remainder after the Shuya transfer.
Kostroma remainder after the Kineshma and Yuryevets transfers.
Retained IISH province geometry.
Retained IISH province geometry.
Oryol remainder after the Bryansk transfer.
Union of the four western Oryol uyezds transferred into Bryansk Governorate.
Added to close a Phase Five omission; retained IISH province geometry.
Added to close a Phase Five omission; retained IISH province geometry.
Retained IISH province geometry.
Reduced Byelorussian SSR territory represented through six Minsk governorate uyezds; the Polish-Soviet control line is only approximated at uyezd resolution.
Union of transferred Minsk, Chernigov, and former Mogilev uyezds; later fine adjustments are not digitized.
Vitebsk remainder after Latvian Latgale transfer.
Soviet-held eastern Volhynian remainder represented by whole uyezds; the Riga frontier cut through prewar administrative space and requires future fine tracing.
The IISH Podolia governorate is retained as the vector basis; the western treaty line remains approximate.
Retained IISH province geometry.
Chernigov remainder after northern uyezds were transferred to Gomel.
Retained IISH province geometry.
Kharkov remainder after eastern uyezds were assigned to Donetsk Governorate.
Ekaterinoslav remainder after the Donbas uyezd transfer.
Union of real Kharkov, Ekaterinoslav, and Don Host uyezds/okrugs; sub-uyezd border changes remain approximate.
Whole-uyezd reconstruction of Nikolayev and northern Taurida transfers; the 1920-1921 internal Ukrainian line needs finer documentary tracing.
Union of the three western Kherson governorate uyezds used for Odessa Governorate.
Crimean peninsula union from the real Taurida district features, excluding mainland Taurida uyezds.
Retained IISH province geometry.
Added to close a Phase Five omission; retained IISH province geometry.
Retained IISH province geometry.
Vyatka remainder after Tatar and Votyak transfers, reconstructed from whole uyezds.
Core autonomous oblast reconstructed from Glazov and Sarapul uyezds; smaller transferred parts remain approximate.
Whole-uyezd union from Kazan, Vyatka, Samara, and Ufa; boundary portions defined below uyezd level remain approximate.
Core autonomous oblast reconstructed from Kozmodemyansk and Tsarevokokshaisk uyezds.
Whole-uyezd reconstruction from Kazan and Simbirsk source features; several partial-uyezd transfers are generalized to their source districts.
Retained IISH province geometry.
Simbirsk remainder after Chuvash transfers.
Samara remainder after Bugulma transfer to the Tatar ASSR.
Saratov remainder after Tsaritsyn transfers.
Whole-district union from Saratov, Astrakhan, and Don Host sources; later boundary refinement remains approximate.
Astrakhan remainder after Tsaritsyn, Kalmyk, and Bukey assignments.
Kalmyk Steppe source feature used as the autonomous oblast basis; small neighboring transfers remain approximate.
Don remainder after Donetsk and Tsaritsyn transfers.
Dissolve of the real Kuban and Black Sea source provinces.
Added to close a Phase Five omission; retained IISH province geometry.
Retained only as a pre-campaign transition record; inactive by the March opening.
Union of core Terek okrugs; Mountain ASSR internal national districts require later fine reconstruction.
Dagestan source province plus adjoining Terek districts; the new ASSR line is reconstructed at district resolution.
Real Kutaisi and Tiflis district union; disputed and treaty frontiers cut below uyezd level and remain explicitly approximate.
Real Erevan and Zangezur district union; Turkish and Azerbaijani treaty frontiers require future fine tracing.
Real Baku, Elisabethpol, Nakhichevan, and Zakatala district union; disputed frontiers remain approximate.
Perm remainder after Ekaterinburg and Chelyabinsk transfers.
Core Ekaterinburg Governorate reconstructed from four Perm uyezds; Shadrinsk is assigned to Chelyabinsk in this March 1921 model.
Whole-uyezd union from Orenburg, Perm, and Tobolsk; fine 1920 transfers remain approximate.
Ufa remainder after Tatar and Bashkir transfers; numerous sub-uyezd changes make the whole-uyezd representation provisional.
Core Bashkir territory reconstructed from whole Ufa uyezds; canton boundaries and partial districts require future fine digitization.
Orenburg capital district and eastern remainder after Chelyabinsk transfers.
IISH Ural region geometry retained and renamed for the Kirghiz ASSR.
The Internal Kirghiz Horde source feature is used directly for Bukey Province.
IISH Turgay region geometry retained and renamed.

administrativePeriods.administrativeType: uyezd territory of Omsk Governorate under Sibrevkom

properties.notes: Stable four-uyezd geographic partition with Omsk district excluded. In March the territory remained under the Siberian Revolutionary Committee as part of Omsk Governorate; from late April it formed Akmolinsk Province in the Kirghiz ASSR.
IISH Semipalatinsk region geometry retained and renamed.
Retained IISH source province geometry.
Retained IISH source province geometry.
Retained IISH source province geometry.
Retained IISH source province geometry.
Retained IISH source province geometry.
Khiva source feature retained as the geographic basis and renamed; republican frontier verification remains at small scale.
Bukhara source feature retained as the geographic basis and renamed; republican frontier verification remains at small scale.
Tobolsk/Tyumen remainder after Omsk and Chelyabinsk transfers.
Omsk Governorate reconstructed from Tara, Tyukalinsk, and Omsk source districts.
Tomsk remainder after the Altai split.
Union of the three southern Tomsk source districts used for Altai Governorate.
Retained IISH province geometry.
Retained IISH province geometry.
Retained IISH source region geometry.
Real Transbaikal source region used for the Far Eastern Republic oblast.
Real Amur source region used for the Far Eastern Republic oblast.
Southern Primorskaya districts separated from the Kamchatka districts.
Northern Primorskaya/Kamchatka source districts dissolved into the Far Eastern Republic unit.
IISH Sakhalin source geometry retained; de facto Japanese occupation is represented by government context rather than altered island geometry.
